observations.recommendation:
1.14. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Inventory Team to complete/finish the conduct of the annual
physical inventory of PPE by type and submit a Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) not later than January
31 of each year.
b) Instruct the Inventory Team to reconcile the RPCPPE with the ledger
balances.
c) Require the GSO-Designate to maintain property cards and reconciled with
the ledger cards of the Municipal Accounting Office.
d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) recognize depreciation expense
monthly on all the PPE accounts; (b) make necessary adjustment in books to
correct the unrecognized depreciation of the seven 7 identified PPE
accounts; and make necessary disclosure on PPEs that are fully depreciated
but still in use; and (c) adopt all the principles prescribed in IPSAS 17 for
proper accounting of the Municipality’s PPE and for a fair presentation of
the asset accounts in the financial statements.

2.13. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Property Supply
Officer or the General Services Officer to follow strictly the accounting and
reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF specifically on the
issuance of inventories/relief goods to ensure the validity and correctness of the
quantity of relief goods per PO and listing of recipients.

3.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should:
a. Strictly require each department head to comply with the monitoring and
reporting of inventories issued and prepare RSMI on a monthly basis and
submit the same to the Municipal Accountant;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant, on the basis of the RSMIs to record
inventory expenses on a monthly basis.
c. Constitute an Inventory Committee which shall conduct physical count of
inventories at least once a year, prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
Inventories and submit the same to the Audit not later than January 31 of each
year;

4.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to classify and record all the procured
supplies and materials from LDRRMF to the appropriate inventory account;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and
Supplies Ledger Cards for all PPE and relief goods in compliance to COA
Circular No. 2014-002 specifically on the provisions pertaining to the
accounting, inventory and monitoring of procured items funded out from the
LDRRMF;
c. Require also the Municipal Treasurer, as the Property Officer to maintain
Property Cards and Stock Cards for monitoring purposes of the consumption
and balances of the inventory of supplies; and
d. Prepare and submit the RSMI to the Municipal Accountant monthly as basis
in recording the expenses in the books of accounts.

5.5 We recommended that management revert the unutilized balance of CY 2013
LDRRMF appropriation back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund
for subsequent re-appropriation by the local sanggunian for other social services.

6.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular reconciliation of the cash book and
SL/GL balances to ensure accuracy of the balances to be reflected in the financial
statements as of a given period.
The Local Chief Executive only approves payment of benefits of employees strictly in accordance with rules and regulations promulgated by competent authority.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC- Human Resource Management Officer to adhere to the provisions set forth in Section 77 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to enforce immediate settlement of the audit disallowances pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2017-021 dated November 3, 2017 and to ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations to the Municipal Mayor:
a. to require the Accounting and Property Divisions to reconcile their
respective books and to maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, to
facilitate reconciliation of PPE.
b. to direct the General Services Officer to regularly conduct complete
physical inventory taking of PPE as well as prepare and submit the
PPE Inventory Report duly reconciled with the accounting records in
accordance with Section 490 of the GAAM and COA Circular No. 80-
124 dated January 18, 1980.

1.10 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the General Services
Officer to undertake proper procedures to properly dispose all obsolete and
unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 379 of R.A. No. 7160 and in
accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89-296. Also,
once proper disposal has been undertaken, the Municipal Accountant should
derecognize in the books said unserviceable PPE items.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the
expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the
LCE or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and employees
concerned to settle their cash advance.
b. cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and
employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed
period pursuant to Section 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 after
being sent notice and demand. The Municipal Accountant is authorized to
delete the name of the official and/or employee from the subsequent payroll
until such time that the cash advance has been fully liquidated pursuant to
COA Circular No. 96-004.
c. direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Budget Officer to refrain from signing cash advance vouchers and
obligation requests of officials and employees with outstanding cash
advances.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant,
Municipal Engineer and Municipal Agriculture to:
a. determine if the specific programs or projects for which the funds were
transferred were already implemented, goods/services were already
delivered, liquidation of funds were already submitted and received by
the source agencies, and liabilities were already settled for proper
adjustments in the books;
b. return to the source agency any unused/unexpended balance to
facilitate the liquidation of the balances for the availment of the benefits
of the projects by the constituents.

We recommended for the licensing of the vehicle with the DOH and since the
ambulance is intended for transporting patients only, we recommend to the
Municipal Mayor to cause the labeling of the vehicle as “PATIENT
TRANSPORT VEHICLE” and shall be registered with the Bureau using the
prescribed form pursuant to DOH A.O. No. 2018-001.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct:
a. the BAC Chairman to initiate appropriate timing/phasing of related
LDRRMF project activities as part of their procurement planning,
specifically the bidding and other related procurement activities, in order
to avoid delays in its implementation.
b. the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Engineer to implement promptly the
PPAs included in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to reduce disaster risks
and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all
levels.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor that proper planning shall be
initiated in the formulation of the AIP which should be in accordance with the
provisions stated under DBM-DILG JMC 2017-01, dated February 22,
2017.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the BAC to fast track the
bidding process and shall be on a “jury duty” until the Notice of Award is
issued by the HOPE in order to complete the entire procurement process at
the earliest possible time.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Budget
Officer to charge wages of JO workers against the MOOE of local projects
authorized by the local sanggunian and not against the MOOE of any office.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer
to intensify collection and send demand letters citing the municipal
ordinance that the non-payment of fees due as provided in the ordinance is
ground for the disconnection of water connection. Also, the Municipal
Engineering Office should conduct regular maintenance of the waterworks
system.

We recommended to the Local Sanggunian that an ordinance on local GAD
Code be enacted to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and
to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated
local development plans.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the GAD Focal Person
to formulate programs and projects and activities through the conduct of
gender analysis to ensure that valid GAD issues are addressed and the full
amount attributed in the GAD Plan and Budget is accurate.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the GAD Focal Person
to adhere to the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEA JMC 2013-01 so that the
intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other
assistance that may be provided.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to compel the Municipal
Accountant to exhaust all possible remedies for purposes of causing the
immediate return of funds granted for livelihood assistance by the LGU.
This could be initiated by sending of collection letters to defaulting debtors
demanding for immediate payment thereof.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to compel the Municipal
Accountant to immediately verify all supporting documents proving that no
actual claims have been filed against the long outstanding Accounts
Payable and revert back to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund.
The reversion, however, shall be carried out after seeking the authority
from COA for the undertaking to be properly and validly carried out
pursuant to relevant law and rules.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to request the Sangguniang
Bayan to enact an ordinance authorizing the grant of RATA, at an amount
authorized by law, to personnel holding OIC positions pursuant to
provisions of RA No. 7160.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant
to reconcile and verify from Philhealth the outstanding unremitted
contributions on the previous years in order to ascertain the accuracy and
reliability of the balances of Due to Philhealth in the financial statements.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to
trace the unremitted balance against the subsidiary ledger or the individual record of
the employees and confirm from Pag-IBIG Office the outstanding unremitted
contributions in the previous years.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to adopt monitoring mechanism to detect variances in the amount
to be remitted from the salaries withheld for the GSIS contributions of
employees.
19.8 Furthermore, we also recommended that accounting records such as cash
receipts journal, check disbursement journal, cash disbursement journal,
general and subsidiary ledgers must be updated monthly in order to
determine the exact amount to be remitted to the GSIS.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant:
a. to exert all effort to locate the documents to support this account;
b. to send demand letters to those employees who have not yet settled
their disallowances; and,
c. to strictly monitor the settlement of suspension and disallowances and
communicate to the Audit Team the specific transaction on this
account that cannot be retrieve anymore.

We recommended that the landfill be fenced with a secured gate at the
entrance to minimize illegal visitors or scavengers on the landfill. This
will also help address the occupational health and safety issues by
limiting access.

22.10 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Treasurer to see to it that checks issued are charged against the correct
depository account.
22.10 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Treasurer to see to it that the correct depository account shall be debited
for any check payments and date of check should not be antedated to
avoid such deficiency. We also recommend that the good practice as
regards to the maintenance of an accurate accounting records for cash in
bank accounts and submission of updated bank reconciliation statements
be sustained.

Considering that the Municipality would continuously secure loans and
borrowings to finance development and investment projects, we
recommended to the Municipal Mayor together with the Sangguniang Bayan
Members, to consider the Municipality’s’ net available funds prior to
contracting of loans/borrowings, that is, not to exceed 20% of the regular
income of the Municipality after allocating the full provision for statutory and
contractual obligations.

observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 Municipal Mayor:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to enforce liquidation of unliquidated cash advances by sending demand letters to persons accountable.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018
observations.reason: remained out of the total ₱352,717.29.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the municipal Assessor in concerted efforts with Municipal Officials concerned to expedite the necessary steps in acquiring the title of the land. We further recommended that before any construction is made, land ownership thru a certificate of title must be obtained.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer and all other officials concerned who are tasked to formulate the annual budget to specifically include the program and projects charged against the 20% DF in the AIP and include only projects/programs that partake the nature of capital expenditures.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer all unexpended balance of LDRRMF as of year-end to the Special Trust Fund account as required to protect the fund against misuse.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider the appropriate means of communication depending on the information that it wishes to pass on to the public and its stakeholders. For instance, have public notices on television and radio about who can access the residual containment area, the working hours at the RCA to limit after-hours access and the schedule for collection of wastes from households for the different zones; and create a specific link for SWM within the Municipality’s website.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Waste Management Focal Person to maintain disposal site records in the RCA.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume and type of wastes disposed be put in place at the site which will provide baseline data to assist in developing effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for waste management.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the RCA be fenced with a secured gate at the entrance to minimize illegal visitors or scavengers. This will also help address occupational health and safety issues by limiting access.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collections of delinquent market stall/space rentals and waterworks service fees in order to augment funds to finance development projects of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account in the general fund for waterworks, and require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain separate bank accounts for waterworks operation.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to submit GAD accomplishment reports and see to it that programs, projects and activities are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that may be provided.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to establish VAW Help Desks in the barangays to be manned by a competent personnel.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Agriculture, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to fast track implementation of various projects funded from fund transfers from source agencies to facilitate the liquidation of the funds as well as the availment of the benefits of the projects by the constituents.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make reconciliation of their records with that of the GSIS. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant shall also verify who among the employees is deficient in paying their loan obligations so that an adjustment in his/her deductions shall be made.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger and verify from Philhealth the outstanding unremitted contributions in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balances of Due to Philhealth in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit intact all taxes withheld. The Municipal Accountant should closely monitor the withholding of taxes while the Municipal Treasurer should remit promptly and intact the same to the BIR to avoid interest and penalties on delayed remittances and accumulation of unremitted taxes in compliance to BIR regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the rendition and submission of the required RAAF of Revenue Collection Officers be strictly complied in order to avoid delay in the preparation of consolidated report on accountability for accountable forms and verification on the correctness could be facilitated.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended appropriate internal procedures and computations regarding borrowing to ensure compliance with limits and consideration of statutory and contractual obligations prior to contracting loans.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
   Accountant to enforce liquidation of cash advances by sending demand
   letters to persons accountable.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
   Assessor in concerted efforts with Municipal Officials concerned  to
   expedite the necessary steps in acquiring  the  title  of  the  land
   owned by the Municipality We further  recommended  that  before  any
   construction is made, land ownership thru  a  certificate  of  title
   must be obtained.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget
   Officer  and  all  other  officials  concerned  who  are  tasked  to
   formulate the annual budget to specifically include the program  and
   projects charged against the 20% DF in  the  AIP  and  include  only
   projects/programs that partake the nature of  capital  expenditures.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal
   Accountant to transfer all unexpended balance of LDRRMF as of  year-
   end to the Special Trust Fund account as  required  to  protect  the
   fund against misuse.

We recommended  that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person
   to consider the appropriate means of communication depending on  the
   information that it  wishes  to  pass  on  to  the  public  and  its
   stakeholders. For  instance,  there  should  be  public  notices  on
   television and on the  radio  about  who  can  access  the  residual
   containment area, the working hours at the RCA to limit  after-hours
   access and the schedule for collection of wastes from households for
   the different zones. Websites have become increasingly effective and
   economical in disseminating information. Therefore it  is  important
   to have a specific link for SWM within the Municipality’s website so
   that it is easier for the public to have access to  information  and
   policy on solid waste management as well  as  any  issue  concerning
   solid waste management.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Solid  Waste
   Management Focal Person to maintain disposal  site  records  in  the
   RCA.

We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume  and  type  of
   wastes should be in place at the site which  will  provide  baseline
   data to assist in developing effective waste minimization strategies
   and future plans for waste management.

We recommended that the RCA be fenced with a  secured  gate  at  the
   entrance to minimize illegal visitors or scavengers. This will  also
   help address the occupational  health  and  safety  issues  by  limiting
   access.

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  cause  the  allocation  of
   funds to purchase the appropriate safety  clothing;  the  SWM  Focal
   Person should ensure that any person entering the RCA must wear  the
   proper safety clothing so that health risks are minimized  and  that
   the standards and processes prescribed in the  manual  are  complied
   with so that the operations at the RCA will be safe.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal
   Treasurer to    enforce collections of delinquent market stall/space
   rentals and waterworks service fees in order  to  augment  funds  to
   finance development projects of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor required: (a) the  Municipal
   Accountant to maintain a special account in  the  general  fund  for
   waterworks,  and, (b) the Municipal Treasurer to  maintain  separate
   bank accounts for waterworks operation.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  OIC-  Human
   Resource Management Officer to adhere to the provisions set forth in
   Section 77 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal  Person
   to submit GAD accomplishment reports and see to  it  that  programs,
   projects and activities are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so
   that  the  intended  beneficiaries  are  assured  of  the  services,
   protection and other assistance that may be provided.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person
   to establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  GAD  Focal
   Person to establish VAW Help Desks in the barangays to  be  manned
   by a competent personnel.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
   Accountant  to  enforce   immediate   settlement   of   the   audit
   disallowances  pursuant  to  COA  Resolution  No.  2017-021   dated
   November 3, 2017 and to ensure that all employees who are  retiring
   or  transferring  to  other  agencies  shall   first   settle   the
   disallowances for which they are liable in accordance with  Section
   7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal
   Engineer, Municipal Agricultrue,  Municipal  Social  Welfare  and
   Development  Officer  and  Municipal  Planning  and   Development
   Officer to fast track  the  implementation  of  various  projects
   funded from fund transfers from source  agencices  to  facilitate
   the liquidation of the funds as well  as  the  availment  of  the
   benefits of the porjects by the constituents.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal
   Accountant to make reconciliation of their records with that of the
   GSIS.  Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant shall also verify  who
   among the employees is deficient in paying their  loan  obligations
   so that an adjustment in his/her deductions shall be made.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal
   Accountant  to  maintain  a  subsidiary  ledger  and  verify  from
   Philhealth the outstanding unremitted contributions  in  order  to
   ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balances of  Due  to
   Philhealth in the financial statements.
The Local Chief Executive to compel the Municipal Accountant together with the GSO Officer to reconcile their respective records.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office has started conducting the actual inventory of all the properties of the LGU.
observations.reason: The GSO is almost done in the preparatio n of the RPCPPE and RPCI.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive to compel the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to COA Audit Team all supporting documents proving the existence of claimants covering the questioned Accounts Payable account, otherwise, the same shall be reverted back to the unappropriated surplus.  The reversion, however, shall be carried out after seeking the intervention of COA for the undertaking to be properly and validly carried out pursuant to relevant law and rules.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed and traced the Accounts Payable from CY 2012 – CY 2017. And entries were prepared for the necessary adjustments to come up with the correct Accounts Payable amount with the corresponding claimants.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive only approves payment of benefits of employees strictly in accordance with rules and regulations promulgated by competent authority the Department of Budget and Management.
observations.managementAction: In December 2018, management has not approved any additional benefits for the employees. All the municipal officials and regular employees agreed to pay in staggered basis until full payment of the amount received or on the date the Notices of Disallowance become final and executory, whichever comes earlier.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive to cause the submission of a justification for non-compliance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1.2017 of the DILG-DBM dated February 22, 2017, requiring the LGU to utilize the 20% component of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for development projects that will contribute to the attainment of a desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes. Moreover, we recommended that the program/project guidelines should indicate its rationale, objectives, significance and strategies of implementation to assist us in determining whether such programs/projects were economically and efficiently utilized.
observations.managementAction: The Programs, Projects and Activities for the 20% Economic Development Fund for CY 2017 were prepared in the same manner with the prior years’ budget which we thought was compliant to the guidelines set by the oversight agencies. As part of the management actions, the undersigned have called for a special meeting to review and/or revise the PPAs for the remaining funds of the 20% EDF for CY 2017 and CY 2018 budgets which do not conform to the provisions under the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017. Upon completion of the revised PPAs, coordination with your office will be done to make sure that the PPAs newly identified are compliant with the set guidelines. The 20% EDF Annual Investment Plan for CY 2018 and the remaining unutilized funds for CY 2017 were already revised and changed to PPAs that conform to the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the program/projects guidelines should indicate its rationale, objectives, significance and strategies of implementation to assist us in determining whether such programs/projects were economically and efficiently utilized.
observations.managementAction: The Programs, Projects and Activities for the 20% Economic Development Fund for CY 2017 were prepared in the same manner with the prior years’ budget which we thought was compliant to the guidelines set by the oversight agencies. As part of the management actions, the undersigned have called for a special meeting to review and/or revise the PPAs for the remaining funds of the 20% EDF for CY 2017 and CY 2018 budgets which do not conform to the provisions under the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017. Upon completion of the revised PPAs, coordination with your office will be done to make sure that the PPAs newly identified are compliant with the set guidelines. The 20% EDF Annual Investment Plan for CY 2018 and the remaining unutilized funds for CY 2017 were already revised and changed to PPAs that conform to the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive to: a) Require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the LCE or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and employees concerned to settle their cash advance. b) Cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and employees who failed to settle their cash advances with the prescribed period pursuant to Section 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 after being sent notice and demand. The Municipal Accountant is authorized to delete the name of the official and/or employee from the subsequent payroll until such time that the cash advance has been fully liquidated pursuant to COA Circular NO. 96-004. c) Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from signing cash advance vouchers and obligation requests of officials and employees with outstanding cash advances. d) Institute measures to demand liquidation of the account by serving notice and demand settlement to the officials/employees concerned.
observations.managementAction: This office has sent final demand letters to the AOs for the immediate submission of the liquidation reports, otherwise, full payment of the same. Meeting with two (2) of the AOs was undertaken and they promised to pay their obligations on staggered basis which started in March 2018. One of these two AOs who have unliquidated CAs was dismissed by the current LCE for Serious Dishonesty, Grave Misconduct, Falsification of Public Document and Conduct prejudicial to the best interest of the service on March 14, 2018. She has been paying her unliquidated CAs thru SALARY DEDUCTIONS. Since the dismissal, she has not paid any amount for the settlement of her obligation.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2018, ₱35,024.06 remained unliquidat ed out of the total ₱352,717.2 9.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive to compel the Municipal Accountant to strictly exhaust all possible remedies for purposes of causing the immediate return of funds granted for livelihood assistance by the LGU. This could be initiated by the sending of collection letters to defaulting debtors demanding for immediate payment thereof.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters were already sent to each of the beneficiaries with the corresponding photocopies of the DVs, Pledge of Undertaking, and Agreements undertaken by the LGU and the beneficiaries. Second demand letters will be sent to those unresponsive debtors this CY 2019.
observations.reason: Payments for CY 2018 amounted to ₱27,000.00.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to utilize the 1% of the IRA of the municipality strictly in accordance with the provisions of Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 known as Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006.
observations.managementAction: A meeting was called on April 5, 2018 for the revision of the LCPC Work & Financial Plan and it was decided that the LGU will send letters to the DILG RO8 for clarification on the matter since our LCPC Work and Financial Plan was formulated in accordance to the guidelines set by the DILG MC No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 entitled ALLOCATION OF 1% IRA for the strengthening and implementation of the PPAs of the LCPC per Section 15 of RA 9344. A letter was received from Ms. Marivel C. Sacendoncillo, CESO III, Regional Director, DILG RO8 and she states that the allocation of 1% of the IRA budget for strengthening the LCPC is not only limited to the identified PPAs per DILG MC 2012-120 or in RA 9344, but to other related laws/policies for the welfare of all children and youth. Thus, addressing the four (4) major rights of children (survival, development, protection and participation) with the ultimate objective: for the “BEST INTEREST OF THE CHILD.”

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to see to it that there should be sufficient appropriation of proposed particular project or expenditure; 2) Require the BAC and MBO to explain in writing why there were two fund sources for the purchase of non-biodegradable shredder and plastic Densifier and why these items were procured when they were not found in the approved Annual Performance Budget for CY 2017, hence, rendering the disbursement irregular.
observations.managementAction: During this year, the LGU has not accommodated purchases and/or disbursements with two fund sources and not found in the approved Annual Performance Budget.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive to cause the submission of a justification for non-compliance with the Joint Memorandum Circular and to direct the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Agriculture, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to fast track the implementation of various projects funded from fund transfers from source agencies to facilitate the liquidation of the funds as well as the availment of the benefits of the projects by the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The liquidation report for the Construction of Dry Market was already submitted to the DILG RO8 in June 2018. The ICFP, ADM, SWIP, and Supplemental Feeding Program and projects are still on-going. While the Infusion of New Breeder Stocks of Turkey, Native Chicken and Swine will be procured through Negotiated Procurement since there were no responsive bidders. And the documents for the change of location and project title for the Livestock Program (Slaughterhouse) will be submitted to the DA RFO8 for approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to see to it that there should be sufficient appropriation of proposed particular project or expenditure; 2) Require the BAC and MBO to explain in writing why there were two fund sources for the purchase of non-biodegradable shredder and plastic Densifier and why these items were procured when they were not found in the approved Annual Performance Budget for CY 2017, hence, rendering the disbursement irregular.
observations.managementAction: During this year, the LGU has not accommodated purchases and/or disbursements with two fund sources and not found in the approved Annual Performance Budget.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive to cause the submission of a justification for non-compliance with the Joint Memorandum Circular and to direct the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Agriculture, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to fast track the implementation of various projects funded from fund transfers from source agencies to facilitate the liquidation of the funds as well as the availment of the benefits of the projects by the constituents.
observations.managementAction: During this year, the LGU has not accommodated purchases and/or disbursements with two fund sources and not found in the approved Annual Performance Budget.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive to direct the GAD council together with the LPDO to come-up with the database in attuning to the demands of GAD issues and concerns.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is still doing the following: 1.BCRS-IS was conducted by the Barangay Secretaries under the supervision of our LCR but the system is not yet fully operational due to some improvements being undertaken; 2. Geo-tagging of households for all barangays was already done; 3. Ongoing CLUP Updating to get disaggregated data; and 4. Integrating the BHW data per household under the supervision of our RHU Personnel.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive should consider creating and organizing an Internal Audit Services, in compliance with R.A. No. 4177 and AO 70 to give assurance for a sound and adequate internal control system in the operations of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU plans to create and organize an Internal Audit Services (IAS) in the CY 2019 Annual Performance Budget. Some LGU Officials attended the Roll-out trainings of BOM, LEEs, IAM manuals for LGUs in Ormoc City in CY 2016 and they already have ideas on the Organization Structure and staffing pattern with the department’s roles and responsibilities and functional statements.
observations.reason: The budget for this Department was not included in the CY 2018 AIP and Annual Performance Budget. The LGU will procure the Internal Audit Manual from the Department of Budget and Management to guide us in the actual creation and organization of the IAS Department.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive to compel the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provisions of the General Appropriations Act so that failures and lapses will be avoided. 2) Require the Municipal Treasurer that the amount withheld shall be remitted to GSIS in full within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the withholding was accrued. 3) Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct thorough reconciliation on affected employees with negative balances for over six months to five years, adjust and reconcile for this calendar year. 4) Compel the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to make all efforts to review, adjust and reconcile for this calendar year. 5) Compel the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to make all efforts to review, adjust and reconcile for this calendar year.
observations.managementAction: The assigned offices cleared the payrolls by excluding our unauthorized deductions from the payrolls with stern instruction not to accommodate deductions for payments of loans to other Financial Institutions (i.e. CFI, NCCB, etc.); This action took effect in March 2018.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management to utilize the LDRRMF in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and direct the Municipal Accountant to create controls in the disbursement of fund for LDRRM.
observations.managementAction: On April 5, 2018, a meeting was convened to tackle on the revision of the PPAs reflected on the 5% LDRRMF AIP for CY 2018 and the remaining unutilized LDRRMF for CY 2017 and prior years. The LDRRMF AIP was finally revised based on the guidelines set by COA and NDRRMC DBM DILG JMC2013-1 3.25.13 on the allocation and utilization of the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant, OIC-Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the utilization and provisions of Section 272 of TA 7160 and the Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 of DepED, DBM and DILG, the Revised Guidelines on the use of the Special Education Fund (SEF).
observations.managementAction: In CY 2018, the LGU has not accommodated any request from the various schools for programs, projects and activities which are not specified in the revised guidelines for the utilization of the SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that regardless of urgency of need, management should strictly adhere to the required payment of checks directly to payees thru compliance with pertinent laws and rules especially those involving procurement being the most contradicted ones. Furthermore, concerned officials should plan recurring even extraordinary expenses prior to the incurrence to avoid resort to reimbursements.
observations.managementAction: This CY 2018, the LGU has not paid any reimbursements of any kind.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive: 1) Require the Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 8291 so that failures and lapses will be avoided. 2) Require the Municipal Treasurer that the amount withheld shall be remitted to GSIS in full within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the withholding was accrued. 3) Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct thorough reconciliation on affected employees with negative balances for over six months to five years, adjust and reconcile for this calendar year. 4) Compel the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to make all efforts to review, adjust and reconcile for this calendar year.
observations.managementAction: This CY 2018, the LGU has already reviewed and reconciled our records with the GSIS data provided and had the following conclusions: 1.Some of the employees affected were those whose Service Records were cut due to separation from the service but were still reflected in the GSIS monthly billings; 2. One employee also was on SICK LEAVE WITHOUT PAY, thus, he should not be paid with monthly contributions but was still in the GSIS billing; and 3. Some of the employees’ contributions were unpaid since those were the payables which occurred during the time of reconciliation. In June 2018, the employee in-charge coordinated with the GSIS for the reconciliation of data with all the supporting documents ready just in case the GSIS needs proof of remittance. After the reconciliation, the unpaid contributions was settled no October 9, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Local Chief Executive continue its plans and programs for Solid Waste Management by: a) Providing the necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRFs and to implement waste segregation at source; b) Strictly enforce and intensify the information campaign on the solid waste reduction among the residents; c) Consider embarking on effective projects and programs to support the LGUs ecological solid waste management program; and d) Strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Members of MSWMB, BESWMC and appointment of BESWM Focal Person updated and activated, meetings and planning workshop conducted to barangay officials and barangay solid waste focal person. Forty-eight (48) MRFs monitored are mostly functional; some are not, Memorandum of Understanding created for signing by the LCE, Amada Amparo (scrap buyer) and Punong Barangay. IEC and distribution of IEC materials was conducted to 29/30 barangays and 2/4 High Schools. Non-collection of un-segregated waste from HH was implemented; conducted composting training to 3 coastal barangays (Malibago, Himatagon, & San Isidro) for biodegradable waste on August 1-3, 2018. IEC materials were distributed during the conduct of 4Ps FDS, Food handlers seminar, Munisipyo sa Baryo, ESWM Focal person workshop, ESWM Summit, 2 IEC materials (RA 9003) posted at terminal area, 4 anti-littering to barangays and highways. Materials for Recycling livelihood (pillow making) turn-over to Ayahag Women’s Association. Materials for procurement, additional MRF & RCA building funded by EMB Shredding of plastics is suspended due to machine breakdown, additional shredder machine donated by EMB, and to procure additional equipment for Municipal Eco park. Piling and packing of waste continued by the 3 final waste sorter at final disposal site. Implementation of Municipal Ordinance 2013-14, Mun. Ordinance No. 2011-10, Ordinance No. 2006-03, tickets issued to violators by the assigned enforcers. Portion of access road concreted (300m both lanes) Competition at barangays was facilitated, for fabrication of portable segregated waste bins as award. Portion of access road concreted (300m both lanes) Implementation of Municipal Ordinance 2013-14, Mun. Ordinance No. 2011-10, Ordinance No. 2006-03, tickets issued to violators by the assigned enforcers. Materials for procurement, additional MRF & RCA building funded by EMB Shredding of plastics is suspended due to machine breakdown, additional shredder machine donated by EMB, and to procure additional equipment for Municipal Eco park.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the amount due to the Pag-Ibig and compel the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile the amounts withheld and remitted to PAG-Ibig in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balances of Due to Pag-ibig in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The personnel in-charge traced the source documents and have found out that: The amount reflected in the September 2013 payroll was only ₱1,200.00 while the amount posted in the ledger was ₱12,000.00; and Newly hired and/or promoted employees’ contributions reflected in the individual Disbursement Vouchers were not remitted unintentionall y. The management has settled the discrepancies on December 7, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive: 1) Direct the Human Resource Management Office to review the duties and functions of the Job Order Workers so as not to duplicate the duties and functions of the regular plantilla employees; and 2) Compel the Municipal Budget Officer to adjust the rates of Job Order Workers in compliance to regulations.
observations.managementAction: The offices tasked to focus on this observation had already undertaken the necessary actions and it was decided that the remaining balance of ₱60.00 per day to meet the required DOLE minimum wage rate will be paid for the months of November and December 2018. These rates will be the same rates that will be used in the next calendar year until further information from the DOLE Regional Office 8 for any increases.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Physical Inventory Committee to: Conduct a complete physical count of PPE; Submit to the Auditor the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly reconciled with the accounting records; List all Property, Plant and Equipment found on hand, classifying them into serviceable or unserviceable; maintain updated property cards per category on all property, plant and equipment as recorded in the list of Property, Plant and Equipment of accounting records; reconcile property records ensuring that the balances shown in the prepared RPCPPE agree with the supply records and the book balances in the accounting records, and vice versa; renew and maintain all Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for the signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared RCPPE.; prepare the necessary Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and Waste Material Report for the unserviceable properties found on hand, taking into consideration EO No. 888 March 18, 1983, COA Memorandum No. 2005-027. Direct the Municipal Assessor to produce a listing of all the properties owned by the government and reconcile them with those properties counted by the committee with an explanation for those items that are recorded in the books but could not be physically located; Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to determine the validity of the PPE account through reconciliation of the accounting records and the actual inventory; Require the Municipal Accountant to: Prepare the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) for each class of property, plant and equipment listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information about the property, plant and equipment, based on the source documents of the transactions; and Drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties after the Waste Material Report and the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property are duly approved and evaluated by COA.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office has already conducted the physical inventory of the LGU properties and on the final phase of preparation of the RPCPPE and RPCI. The Municipal Assessors Office is currently processing on the documents needed for the transfer of titles of the real properties owned by the LGU. The Municipal Accountant Office was finalized in February 2016, thus, the changes and adjustments reflected in the reports were not also being reflected in the GSO report. The accounting office has sent confirmation letters to all the departments for verification as to the existence and usage of various PPEs, thus, the unserviceable properties were removed from the list of PPEs reported in the FS. The GSO report which is based on the conducted actual physical inventory includes those that were still serviceable acquired through purchase or donations from the previous recorded PPE up to December 31, 2015. However, these do not include the unserviceable (waste materials) and unrecorded equipments from the previous administrations which are different from the Accounting records that are cumulative in nature. On the other hand, the Inventory report of the GSO was based on the physical inventory as of December 31, 2015 of the purchases from Procurement Service and through bidding. This report does not include other purchases through petty cash. Copies of the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) were not attached to the RPCPPE but in the remarks portion of the report, there were notations that some equipments were issued to concerned employees but the corresponding ARE Number was not indicated. It has always been practiced that whenever equipment are issued, pre-numbered AREs are signed by the concerned employees and copies are furnished to them. The result of this reconciliation will be reflected to the revised report using the correct classification and valuation of PPE and Inventory accounts in accordance to the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).
observations.reason: The offices concerned have not yet fully completed the necessary documents for the transfer of titles and submission of the RPCI and RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive is recommended to cause the submission of a justification for non-compliance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 series of DILG and DBM dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO has conducted series of reviews on the PPAs enumerated in the 20% EDF Budget for CY 2017 and these PPAs were already in conformance to the PPAs listed in the guidelines. Some of these PPAs were already implemented while some are to be continually implemented in CY 2019 due to time constraint. All the PPAs sourced out of the 20% EDF are already in accordance to the guidelines set forth by law.

observations.recommendation: Strictly utilize the results of the physical count of PPE and Inventories in reconciling the accounts by both the Municipal Accountant and GSO; Provide for Lapsing Schedules for PPE with suggested data as mentioned in the preceding paragraphs by the Accountant; Rectify/correct the classification of accounts in RPCPPE as prescribed in PPSAS for PPEs; 4. Provide schedules for Land as an attachment in the Statement of Financial Position and conduct physical count of Land which will also form part in the Physical Count of PPE by the GSO, Accountant and Inventory Committee.
observations.managementAction: The lapsing schedule for depreciation of PPEs was prepared by the Municipal Accountant in January 2016 and was not required to be submitted since, until now. It has always been her basis for the computation of the Depreciation Expenses for all the properties of the LGU. The RPCPPE and RPCI were submitted in January 2016 and the report of the Municipal Accounting Office was finalized in February 2016, thus, the changes and adjustments reflected in the reports were not also being reflected in the GSO report. The accounting office has sent confirmation letters to all the departments for verification as to the existence and usage of various PPEs, thus, the unserviceable properties were removed from the list of PPEs reported in the FS. The GSO report which is based on the conducted actual physical inventory includes those that were still serviceable acquired through purchase or donations from the previous recorded PPE up to December 31, 2015. However, these do not include the unserviceable (waste materials) and unrecorded equipments from the previous administrations which are different from the Accounting records that are cumulative in nature. On the other hand, the Inventory report of the GSO was based on the physical inventory as of December 31, 2015 of the purchases from Procurement Service and through bidding. This report does not include other purchases through petty cash. Copies of the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) were not attached to the RPCPPE but in the remarks portion of the report, there were notations that some equipments were issued to concerned employees but the corresponding ARE Number was not indicated. It has always been practiced that whenever equipment are issued, pre-numbered AREs are signed by the concerned employees and copies are furnished to them. The result of this reconciliation will be reflected to the revised report using the correct classification and valuation of PPE and Inventory accounts in accordance to the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).
observations.reason: The GSO is still on the finalizati on phase on the RPCPPE and RCPI.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and Treasurer, to follow strict compliance to relevant laws and rules governing the administration of LDRRMF for government resources to be assured of having been administered and used properly, regularly and legally according to the purposes for which the fund was created.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2016-2017, the LGU has transferred to the Special Trust Calamity Fund account the unutilized 30% QRF and the remaining funds were added to the continuing appropriations for disbursement in the succeeding year.

observations.recommendation: Management should adhere strictly to Section 89 of P.D. 1445; COA Circular 97-002 specifically Sections 5.7 and 8, and COA Circular 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 in the implementation of the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Enforce sanctions in case of failure of the accountable officer to liquidate his/her cash advances on time. Direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor the liquidation of cash advances granted to officers/employees and prohibit the grant of additional cash advance pending liquidation of the previous cash advance. Institute measures to demand liquidation of the account by serving notice and demand settlement to the officials/employees concerned.
observations.managementAction: Management has already sent Office Memorandum, three (3) call slips and demand letters subsequently to those accountable officers who did not take action for the liquidation of their cash advances. At present, the management doesn’t grant additional cash advances to officials and employees unless the prior cash advances are settled, except for those special cases. The management has started the collection of the unliquidated cash advances through salary deductions ₱500.00 or ₱1,000.00 per pay day. As of December 31, 2018, ₱35,024.06 remained unliquidat ed out of the total ₱739,990.50. On the other hand, the management has filed appropriate administrative actions against those delinquent accountable officers.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2018, ₱35,024.06 remained unliquidat ed out of the total ₱739,990.5 0.

observations.recommendation: Management should strictly implement the provisions of the Pledge of Undertakings for loans assistance extended to the local residents. We further recommend that the Local Chief Executive issue demand letters to all delinquent borrowers/beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Management already issued demand letters to the said beneficiaries and conducted series of meetings for the recovery of such funds. The Municipality is now filing legal actions to those unresponsive beneficiaries.
observations.reason: The agency is still on the process of collecting from the beneficiar ies their payments of the livelihood assistance provided to them. Payments for CY 2018 amounted to ₱27,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Accountant together with the GSO officer to reconcile their respective records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Accountant to immediately submit to the COA Audit Team all supporting documents
proving the existence of claimants covering the questioned Accounts Payable
account, otherwise, the same shall be reverted back to the unappropriated surplus.
The reversion, however, shall be carried out after seeking the intervention of COA for
the undertaking to be properly and validly carried out pursuant to relevant law and
rules.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive only approve payment of
benefits of employees strictly in accordance with rules and regulations promulgated
by competent authority, the Department of Budget and Management.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the submission of
justification for non-compliance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2017
of the DILG-DBM, requiring the LGU to utilize the 20% component of the Internal
Revenue Allotment (IRA) for development projects that will contribute to the
attainment of a desirable socio-economic development and environmental
management outcomes.

Moreover, we recommended that local officials should ensure that the
programs/projects included in the agency’s Annual Investment Plan for the 20%
Development Fund are in consonance with the development projects listed under the
DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2017 dated February 22, 2017.

We also recommended that the programs/project guidelines should indicate
its rationale, objectives, significance and strategies of implementation to assist us in
determining whether such programs/projects were economically and efficiently
utilized.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the
expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of
the LCE or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and
employees concerned to settle their cash advance.
b) Cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and
employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed
period pursuant to Section 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 after
being sent notice and demand. The Municipal Accountant is authorized to
delete the name of the official and/or employee from the subsequent
payroll until such time that the cash advance has been fully liquidated
pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-004.
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Budget Officer to refrain from signing cash advance vouchers and
obligation requests of officials and employees with outstanding cash
advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Accountant to strictly exhaust all possible remedies for purposes of causing the
immediate return of funds granted for livelihood assistance by the LGU. This could
be initiated by the sending of collection letters to defaulting debtors demanding for
immediate payment thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget
Officer and Municipal Accountant to utilize the 1% of the IRA of the municipality
strictly in accordance with the provisions of Section 15 of Republic Act 9344
known as Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Budget Officer
(MBO) to see to it that there should be sufficient appropriation of proposed
particular project or expenditure;
2. Require the BAC and MBO to explain in writing why there were two fund
sources for the purchase of non-biodegradable shredder and plastic densifier and
why these items were procured when they were not found in the approved Annual
Performance Budget for CY 2017, hence, rendering the disbursement irregular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Engineer, Municipal Agriculture, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer
and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to fast track the implementation of
various projects funded from fund transfers from source agencies to facilitate the
liquidation of the funds as well as the availment of the benefits of the projects by the
constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD council
together with the LPDO to come-up with the database in attuning to the demands of
GAD issues and concerns.

The Local Chief Executive should consider creating and organizing an
Internal Audit Services, in compliance with R.A. No 4177 and AO 70 to give
assurance for a sound and adequate internal control system in the operations of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Accountant and Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provisions of the General
Appropriations Act.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction Management to utilize the LDRRMF in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and direct the Municipal
Accountant to create controls in the disbursement of funds for LDRRM.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant, OIC-Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to strictly
observe the utilization and provisions of Section 272 of R.A. 7160 and the Joint
Circular No. 1, s. 2017 of DEPED, DBM and DILG, the Revised Guidelines on the
use of the Special Education Fund (SEF).

We recommended that regardless of urgency of need, management should
strictly adhere to the required payment of checks directly to payees thru compliance
with pertinent laws and rules especially those involving procurement being the most
contradicted ones. Furthermore, concerned officials should plan recurring even
extraordinary expenses prior to their incurrence to avoid resort to reimbursements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Require the Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the provisions of RA
8291 so that failures and lapses will be avoided.
1. Require the Municipal Treasurer that the amount withheld shall be
remitted to GSIS in full within 10 days following the end of the month the
withholding was made or the withholding was accrued.
2. Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct
thorough reconciliation on affected employees with negative balances for
over six months to five years, and request from GSIS for refund.
3. Compel the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to make all
efforts to review, adjust and reconcile for this calendar year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue its plans and
programs for Solid Waste Manage by: (a) providing the necessary support in
enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRFs and to implement waste
segregation at source; (b) strictly enforce and intensify the information campaign on
the solid waste reduction among the residents; (c) consider embarking on effective
projects and programs to support the LGUs ecological solid waste management
program; and (d) strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines
under RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer
to immediately remit the amount due to the Pag-Ibig and compel the Municipal
Accountant to regularly reconcile the amounts withheld and remitted to Pag-ibig in
order ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balances of Due to Pag-ibig in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

1. Direct the Human Resource Management Office to review the duties and
functions of the Job Order Workers so as not to duplicate the duties and
functions of the regular plantilla employees;
2. Compel the Municipal Budget Officer to adjust the rates of Job Order Workers
in compliance to regulations
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal Accountant together with the GSO officer to reconcile their respective records.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer commented during the exit conference conducted on March 9, 2018 that a complete Physical Count of all PPEs of the LGU shall be made and the Municipal Accountant promised to reconcile its records with the GSO.
observations.reason: The lapsing Partially Implemented. The GSO is still on the finalization of the RPCI and RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: 1. Enhance tax collection via vigorous tax information campaign and intensify tax collection efforts; 2. Exhaust tax remedies as provided by law at the municipal level thru the issuance of demand letters to delinquent taxpayers and posting of notices of delinquency in conspicuous places in the municipal hall and make representations with the Provincial Treasurer to institute the corresponding tax remedies; and 3. Recognize Real Property Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year by recording it in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised that she will correct her actions. To date, the Municipal Treasurer’s and Assessor’s offices have been jointly conducting massive tax information campaign, in all the barangays of the municipality and will conduct a house-to-house tax collection campaign starting this second (2nd) week of April 2016. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office has been sending demand letters to all tax delinquent taxpayers and has posted the same in the local newspaper and Compromise Agreement was also granted to delinquent taxpayers who prayed for an installment tax payments.
observations.reason: The lapsing schedule for depreciation of PPEs was required to be submitted since, until now. It has always been her basis for the computation of Depreciation Expenses for all properties of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer, the Accountant and Treasurer, to follow strict compliance to relevant laws and rules governing the administration of the LDRRMF for assured having resources and used properly, regularly and legally according to the purposes for which the fund was created; and to submit the utilization reports to COA together with the reply of the AOM.
observations.managementAction: For the CY 2015, the remaining unutilized LDRRMF as of December 31, 2015 amounting to ₱1,908,306.42 was not transferred to STCF since no more cash is available for the transfer of the unutilized LDRRMF. The unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for CY 2016, the LGU has transferred ONE MILLION PESOS to the STCF Account on November 10, 2016 per Check No. 815502. The unutilized LDRRMF will be transferred to the STCF in February 2017.
observations.reason: The CY 2015 schedule for the transfer of titles and preparation of the RPCI and RPCPPE was not finalized; thus the changes reflected in the reports were not being reflected in the GSO report. The GSO report which is based on the conducted actual physical inventory includes those that were still serviceable acquired through purchase or donations from the previous recorded PPE up to December 31, 2015. However, these do not include the unserviceable (waste materials) and unrecorded equipments from the previous administrations which are different from the Accounting records that are cumulative in nature.

observations.recommendation: Enforce sanctions in case of failure of the accountable officer to liquidate his/her cash advances on time; direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor the liquidation of cash advances granted to officers/employees and prohibit the grant of additional cash advances to officials and employees unless the previous cash advances are settled; institute measures to demand liquidation of the account by serving notice and demand settlement to the officials/employees concerned.
observations.managementAction: At present, the management doesn’t grant additional cash advances to officials and employees unless the prior cash advances are settled, except for those special cases. The management has started the collection of the beneficiaries' payments through salary deductions. As of December 31, 2017, ₱55,192.29 remained out of the total ₱739,990.50.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented. The LGU has not yet fully implemented the procedures for the liquidation of previous cash advances but has started collection through salary deductions.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive should issue demand letters to all delinquent borrowers/beneficiaries and conduct legal actions against non-responsive borrowers to collect outstanding balances; prioritize collection of the loans and ensure proper monitoring.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is now filing legal actions to those unresponsive borrowers/beneficiaries. Instructions were given to the Municipal Accountant and Public Employment Service Office (PESO) to give priority on the collection of the Loans Receivable from various debtors which remained outstanding since CY 2009.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented. The LGU has started collection efforts and demand letters were issued but full recovery has not yet been realized; some beneficiaries remain unresponsive.

observations.recommendation: 1. Conduct a complete LGU physical inventory and submit the RPCPPE and RPCI to the Auditor; 2. Direct the Municipal Assessor to produce a listing of all properties owned by the government and reconcile them with those counted by the committee; 3. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to determine the validity of the PPE account through reconciliation of the accounting records and the actual inventory; 4. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) for each class of property and drop from the books the unserviceable properties after duly approved Waste Material Report and Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Services Office conducted the physical inventory and submitted the RPCPPE and RPCI. The Municipal Assessor is processing documents needed for transfer of titles owned by the LGU. The Municipal Accountant will prepare PPELC and drop unserviceable items after proper reports are approved by COA.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented. The GSO has conducted physical inventory and submitted reports but reconciliation with accounting records and finalization of some documentation (titles, ARE attachments) remain in process.

observations.recommendation: Require the Bids and Awards Committee, the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer and Budget Officer to strictly follow the requirements on the submission of documents and supporting documents and refrain from paying the said transactions if deficiencies and/or incomplete documents were discovered.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office has already attached the necessary supporting documents to the 1st billing for the contracted infrastructure projects and the lacking documents were already attached to our reply to this audit observation.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented. Supporting documents were attached to the 1st billing and replies were furnished but some documentary deficiencies previously noted remained until corrected.

observations.recommendation: a. Install and monitor a sound internal checks for the control of payees and the advances for payrolls; b. Stop from accommodating/accepting transaction outside the jurisdiction of the local government; c. Stop from issuing check to one of the payees in the payroll, payments must be made by the Disbursing Officer; d. Complete all supporting documents before processing payments to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Disbursing Officer has stopped issuing checks in the name of one payee and now issues separate payments to individual payees. The Municipal Accounting Office stopped accommodating payments outside the jurisdiction and ensured completeness of supporting documents before processing payments.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented. Some improvements were adopted; however, issues remain with prior disbursements and documentation.

observations.recommendation: Provide financial and technical assistance for the establishment and operation of functional and standard-based MRFs in the barangays; conduct wide dissemination of information on source reduction, recycling and composting; implement Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan; construct MRFs and enter into MOAs with scrap buyers for operationalization.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) implemented activities: approval of the 10-year Ecological SWM Plan on May 2017, implementation of MRF construction by 28 barangays, MOAs with scrap buyers, mass IEC, barangay assemblies and distribution of IEC materials.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented. Some barangays still have not constructed their MRFs; implementation is ongoing and some activities were done but full compliance not yet achieved.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive should ensure utilization of the LDRRMF for disaster risk reduction activities and prepare and submit the Investment Plan and utilization reports and ensure transfer of unexpended balances to the Special Trust Calamity Fund as required.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRM Council conducted meetings and implemented projects (repair of seawall, purchase of rescue vehicle counterpart) and transferred ONE MILLION PESOS to the STCF account on Nov 10, 2016 per Check No. 815502.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented. Some transfers and project implementations were done but not all unexpended balances were timely transferred and some reporting requirements remained outstanding.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive is recommended to cause the submission of a justification for non-compliance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2011 of the DILG-DBM dated April 13, 2011. The Budget Officer should always see to it that the PPAs of the 20% DF be in conformance to the Joint Memorandum Circular provisions and the MPDO to conduct timely review of the PPAs for full implementation of the PPAs and full utilization of the funds.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO conducted series of reviews on the PPAs of the 20% DF Budget for CY 2017 and these PPAs were already in conformance to the guidelines. Some PPAs were implemented while others were scheduled for CY 2018. LGU department heads agreed to conduct timely review and implement the PPAs.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented. Some PPAs were implemented but others ran out of time to implement during the year; full compliance to the JMC was not yet achieved.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant, Budget Officer and Treasurer to strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160 and Joint Circular No. 01, s.1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG and Item 4 of Joint Circular No. 1, s.2017 in the utilization of the SEF for allowable purposes.
observations.managementAction: The school heads submitted explanations on questioned disbursements. In CY 2017, the LGU adopted stricter compliance to the government guidelines for proper utilization of the SEF.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented. The LGU has taken steps to observe guidelines but some questioned expenditures required explanations and adjustments.

observations.recommendation: Require the BAC Secretariat to strictly follow the requirements on the submission of the perfected contracts and other supporting documents within five (5) days from executing thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 87-278 dated November 1, 1987 and COA Circular No. 2009-01 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat is currently in-charge on the submission of these documents to COA within the prescribed period and has already submitted the documents to COA as required. The LGU has attached the necessary documents in response to the audit observation.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat is in-charge on the submission of the prescribed documents to COA within the prescribed period. The documents were submitted.
